Petition Concerning The Internal Affairs Of A Trust, Objecting To Accounting And For Return Of Trust
Off Calendar Mar-09-2020 Continued To Jun-22-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
James Lamping for Michael Chaplin, Petitioner.
Steven Braccini for Annell Brickley, Respondent/Trustee.

Mr. Lamping stated his update to the court is that discovery was put on hold for parties to go to mediation, that previous mediation dates were cancelled due to the following: 12/6 mediator had to go to a funeral, 1/10 he got sick and was hospitalized, and 2/14 his client had pneumonia and was contagious, and that everyone is frustrated by the 5 months wasted for mediation. The Court noted that it does not resolve in piecemeal, and that appropriately framed petitions and motions need to be filed to streamline. Mr. Braccini stated they are committed to resolving the case, would like the matter adjudicated, that discovery can be completed in 60 days and request to return in June for trial setting. Mr. Lamping stated that his client remains open to the mediation process, and that he needs to do full discovery.

The Petition concerning the internal affairs of a trust, etc. is continued to June 22, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. A joint status report is to be provided to address status of mediation, any outstanding discovery issues and timelines, what the claims are, dispute of facts, who the witnesses will be, the overall time estimate of the evidentiary hearing, and with the expectation of substantial progress.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: S. Le. Deputy: K. Tauscher. Court Reporter: Katy Leonard CSR 11599 Tel: 415-834-1114, email: reporter@scanlanstone.com
